savedDefaultContract.visibility: The saved outer and inner radii collapse the generated star cross-sections to zero area. Blender and GN-VM retain the exact topology, but the authored default has no useful visible surface.

authoredShaderContract.colorPath: Geometry Attribute col Color → Emission Color
authoredShaderContract.strengthPath: Geometry Attribute power Vector → Emission Strength
authoredShaderContract.browserResolution: GN-VM preserves both evaluated attributes through the star curve, sweep, instancing, and realization path. The attribute-emission material consumes them from the single flat.nodes draw group.

capture.toneMapping: NoToneMapping preserves the extracted linear emission field in the authored evidence capture; it does not claim identical Eevee and WebGL color management.

interpretation: The non-degenerate preview validates geometry and emission-field transport: the WebGL and Blender surfaces align at 0.984 IoU with sub-pixel corner agreement. Because the preview uses one nearly uniform emissive value and the PNGs use different transparent-background mattes, luminance correlation is not a useful shader-structure metric here. The remaining mean-luminance difference is renderer color-management parity, not a geometry or attribute loss.
